Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: Teaches core tools for "economic analysis," like "equilibrium analysis" and "optimization," fundamental to understanding economic productivity and growth (Targets 8.1, 8.2).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: Enhances technological capability by teaching "computing tools" and "modern statistical packages," supporting innovation and research in economic fields (Target 9.5).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: As a "Public Finance" course, it provides "economic analysis" tools to evaluate fiscal policies aimed at achieving greater equality (Target 10.4).

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The "Public Finance" context and "economic analysis" tools can be used to improve the effectiveness and accountability of public financial institutions (Target 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The "Public Finance" curriculum provides analytical tools directly relevant to domestic resource mobilization (tax) and enhancing macroeconomic stability through policy coordination (Targets 17.1, 17.13).
